reviews.review_description: This game with just the music is quite relaxing but it is also challenging to master especially with almost Zero Gravity and each level gives you less fuel to burn. Graphics are simple but great and the silence of space is quite deafening. Great game to play!

What a fun experience, seeing the little lander fly around in zero G! This game has intuitive controls, nice graphics, and it nails the atmosphere of how I image space should feel like. The challenges of landing while using the least possible amount of fuel are really satisfying to complete. There are also some fun extras, like a little race you can do with the lander and even walk around on the surface of the Moon!
I wish developers keep working on this and maybe add a mode where I can sit inside the lander as it's on its way down to the surface!

reviews.review_title: Great Addition To My Toy Box
reviews.review_description: Lunar lander was my favorite game back in the arcade days. This version is way more of a challenge in that it is 3D instead of flat screen. The spacecraft is really tight looking and I’ve always wanted to walk on the moon. It’s very cool and is now one of my go-to’s for VR fun.
Honestly, it made me feel like I am a child again, playing with my toys, except it was way cooler because I did not have a remote controlled moon lander.
This is a really fun experience. The user experience is smooth, and the overall mechanics feel well thought out.
Really nice experience! I really enjoy playing it. Always loved moon gravity

Brought back memories of playing Lunar Lander on Windows 3.1.

Easy to pick up once you figure out the angles and very satisfying to nail a perfect landing.whilst moving around to the landing site to get the perfect view.

I would love to be able to control the pitch 1:1 with the movement of your controller.

A real solid little game!

reviews.review_title: Hard to Master, Impossible to Quit
reviews.review_description:
The core objective—carefully piloting and delicately landing a lunar module on a miniature moon landscape—is both simple and brilliantly executed. It’s incredibly addictive thanks to the ultra-precise and responsive controls. I’ve crashed an absurd number of times, yet I keep coming back for “just one more attempt.”

The progression system with unlockables keeps things feeling fresh and rewarding. I also really appreciate the bonus modes—being able to walk on the moon using teleportation is a great touch, and the race mode adds an exciting layer of challenge.

Looking forward to seeing if a leaderboard gets added—this game is begging for one. It’s the kind of experience that stirs up just enough anxiety to make you want to master the controls and prove yourself. Competitive players are going to love it.

Reminded me of the Lunar Lander/Lunar rescue games I played as a kid.  Really fun to do in VR and they have all sorts of challenges to keep it interesting.  Hope the devs keep on adding even more challenges in the future.

Lander VR is a lot of fun and provides challenges that keep your ambitions high. The controls are very intuitive so that you can start playing immediately and focus on the challenges of landing and navigating the lunar lander rather than spending time on learning "how to play". You can learn to master the mechanics of the lander gradually by completing the levels. This game really doesn't waste time and space with bulk and overhead. It's really play-focused and really keeps you hooked in trying to improve your skills. Its both relaxing and tense, trying to navigate the lunar landscape from your bedroom. Much recommended!

This game has taught me something about myself: I am terrible at landing on the moon. I've lost count of the number of landers I've crashed, but the experience is so engaging that I keep coming back for just one more attempt.
A very fun, engrossing, and challenging casual

At first, I struggled a bit with the controls and wasn’t sure if I’d enjoy the game. But after a few minutes, things started to click, and it became surprisingly intuitive. The calming music really adds to the atmosphere, and the gameplay has a nice, addictive flow. A great little VR experience that rewards a bit of patience!
Beautifully crafted experience piloting the lander across the barren lunar surface with limited fuel. Immersive, challenging, addictive, and a ton of fun!
I'm enjoying this game quite a bit! It's fairly tricky and takes some time to figure out and therefore is addictive!
